genre: Idle-action deckbuilder / real-time card battler / meta-progression collection game

product_direction.identity: Stay firmly in the idle-action deckbuilder space rather than becoming a traditional dense TCG simulator.
product_direction.run_structure: Mostly linear dungeons for now, with more stages and content routes later.
product_direction.progression_goal: Break typical TCG-like rules piece by piece through stage unlocks, automation, and major upgrades.
product_direction.content_priority: Prioritize content breadth and variety first, deeper systems second.
product_direction.power_curve: Fast satisfying power climb with a large amount of long-term content.

architecture.root: Single monolithic App component. Gameplay sub-components and render functions should remain inside App() unless there is a strong reason not to.
architecture.asset_paths: Static assets and CSV files should be resolved via import.meta.env.BASE_URL. Use getAssetPath() when loading from public/ for GitHub Pages compatibility.
architecture.viewport: Desktop framing targets a 16:9 game viewport with black letterboxing.

progression_model.stage_system: Locked future features are hidden until their stage/feature is unlocked.
progression_model.dungeon_notation: Dungeon progression uses floor-room notation X-Y, where every 10 rooms increments the floor.
progression_model.unlock_room_rule: If a feature is already unlocked, its unlock room is replaced with a normal encounter when the run map is built.

CARD_DB.structure: { id, name, type, cost, color, value, desc, icon, optional manaBonus/multiHit/atkBuff/isHeal }

skills.desc: Skill tree nodes are stage-gated for visibility and use dependency-based unlocking.

features: Monster timer ticks up and deals damage when reaching maxTimer
Player mana regens based on meta.regenRate
autoDraw and autoPlayMana logic execute in the combat loop
activeEffects, enemyAttackEffects, floatingDrops, deathEffect, and draw animations are time-filtered

power_buff.current_rule: Temporary attack power buffs do not carry to the next enemy.

working_features: Map progression through stage-defined room lists
recent_vfx_and_ui_progress: Normal draw uses ghost-card motion without hologram frame effects
Draw-card effects use ghost-card motion plus hologram overlays
Enemy death effect iterated into a stronger overload-pop style
Combat VFX particle count is capped to reduce long-session slowdown
Global FX overlay is localized to the game frame
Skill tree opens centered on the central node
Codex upgrade effect alignment follows the preview card bounds
Desktop rendering is framed to a 16:9 viewport with black letterboxing

current_focus.main_goal: Carry stage-based progression from scaffolding into fuller stage content while preserving combat feel and mobile performance.
next_likely_tasks: Define Stage 2 feature placement in dungeon data
Add the next banner/archetype pack such as mill/discard
Continue profiling DOM-based overlay effects on long mobile sessions

known_issues: No single blocking presentation bug is currently the main issue.
Long-session mobile performance may still need more profiling if DOM-based overlay effects accumulate noticeable cost.
strict_constraints: Do NOT use external image URLs. Use lucide-react icons only.
Do NOT extract gameplay sub-components outside the main App() function unless absolutely necessary.
Do NOT create random verification output folders under the project root. Reuse .build-check for build verification.
Do NOT use npx tailwindcss init in this project.
